meta_title: For Freelancers: design with AI to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Design with AI to Solve Debugging Bottlenecks
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken builds. Learn how to use AI-driven design patterns and Claude Code to debug complex apps faster than ever as a freelancer.
content.answer_block: Stop treating debugging as a manual line-by-line chore. Instead, leverage AI to re-architect your design logic. By using Antigravity for structural visualization and Claude Code for iterative error resolution, you shift from 'fixing bugs' to 'optimizing systems,' allowing you to ship high-quality, production-ready code in half the time.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and you’ve been staring at a stack trace for three hours. As a freelancer, your value isn't just in writing code—it's in your velocity. When you hit a wall that feels impossible to debug, your profit margins evaporate and your reputation takes a hit. We’ve all been there: the 'spaghetti code' trap where fixing one bug births three more.

But what if you stopped debugging and started designing? By integrating AI-first workflows, you transform your development process from a reactive struggle into a proactive, architectural exercise. This isn't just about patching errors; it's about using intelligent agents to enforce design integrity, ensuring that your code is not only functional but robust and maintainable from the very first commit.

steps.title: Abstract the Failure State
steps.description: Before touching code, use Antigravity to map the logical flow of the failing feature. This isolates the logic from the syntax errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided codebase and generate a high-level sequence diagram for the broken feature, highlighting potential logical bottlenecks and state management conflicts.

steps.title: Automate Root Cause Identification
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a semantic analysis of your stack trace to identify if the issue is a design flaw rather than a simple syntax error.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the trace provided by the CLI. Explain why this logic fails under high-concurrency conditions using a design-first perspective.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze these errors and map them to our architectural design patterns'

steps.title: Refactor via Design-Driven Prompting
steps.description: Don't ask the AI to 'fix' the code. Ask it to 're-implement' the feature according to a clean design pattern, such as the Repository or Strategy pattern.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this module using the Strategy pattern to decouple the business logic from the error-prone data fetching layer.

steps.title: Inject Defensive Test Suites
steps.description: Before verifying, generate unit tests that specifically target the edge cases that caused the initial failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest test suite that covers the edge cases identified in the flow analysis, ensuring the new design handles null states gracefully.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate and execute test suite for the refactored module'

steps.title: Verify and Validate with AI Agents
steps.description: Run a final validation pass to ensure the architectural changes haven't introduced regressions elsewhere in the dependency graph.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Compare the current system state against the original design diagram. Confirm that all state transitions are now deterministic.

pro_tips: Treat your error logs as data, not noise; feed them into Claude Code as JSON to help the model identify recurring patterns.
Always use a 'Design-First' prompt prefix—it forces the LLM to consider the architecture before writing a single line of code.
Use Antigravity to visualize dependencies before refactoring; never modify a module without knowing its impact on downstream services.
Keep your Claude Code sessions scoped to specific features to prevent the context window from becoming diluted with irrelevant file data.
When a bug persists, ask the AI to 'critique your own solution'—it often reveals hidden assumptions the model made in the previous iteration.
content.next_steps: Once your build is stable, document the architectural shift in your README and use it as a selling point for your next client project.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting hours on manual debugging and start delivering high-velocity, professional-grade code that makes your clients wonder how you do it so fast. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
